narrative:
First Warn Weather Headlines – Wednesday, May 13, 2026

Sunny, pleasant, and seasonable conditions return today.
Cooler overnight temperatures tonight with some rural valleys dipping into the upper 30s.
Warmer and more humid air builds back in for the weekend.
While occasional showers and thunderstorms are possible Friday through Sunday, most of the weekend will not be a washout.
Better thunderstorm chances, including the potential for stronger storms, may arrive early next week.

A refreshing stretch of weather settles back into Missouri and Illinois today behind yesterday’s cold front. The front continues pushing east this morning as low pressure spins across the Great Lakes region, but the colder air associated with this system remains focused farther north and east. For us locally, temperatures will simply ease back closer to normal levels for mid-May.

Expect a beautiful Wednesday across the bi-state region with plenty of sunshine and afternoon highs mainly in the middle 70s. Northerly winds will gradually diminish through the day as surface high pressure slides east out of Nebraska and toward the Mississippi Valley. Humidity levels will remain low, making for another comfortable spring afternoon across the St. Louis Metro, central Missouri, southeast Missouri, and southern Illinois.

Tonight could become one of the coolest nights we’ve seen in several days. Winds will go calm under mainly clear skies, allowing temperatures to cool efficiently after sunset. Most locations will fall into the 40s by Thursday morning, although a few sheltered valleys and rural locations could briefly dip into the upper 30s. Some patchy fog may also develop in low-lying areas by sunrise.

By Thursday, southerly winds begin returning and temperatures will slowly start climbing again as warmer air pushes back into the Midwest. That warming trend becomes much more noticeable heading into Friday and the weekend as upper-level ridging shifts east and Gulf moisture begins expanding northward.

Temperatures this weekend are expected to run above normal with highs likely reaching the 80s area-wide. While some forecast guidance has hinted at near 90-degree temperatures, current trends continue suggesting that widespread 90s are unlikely at this time. Humidity, however, will definitely become more noticeable with dewpoints climbing into the 60s and perhaps near 70 degrees at times.

As for rain chances, there will be opportunities for scattered showers and thunderstorms from Friday through the weekend, but this does not appear to be a complete washout. In fact, there should be far more dry hours than wet ones. The challenge right now is timing smaller disturbances moving through the flow aloft, which will determine exactly when storms develop and where they track. Forecast confidence remains somewhat lower on those finer details.

Looking ahead into early next week, the pattern may become more active as a larger storm system develops over the western United States and moves eastward. That setup could bring a more organized chance for showers and thunderstorms Monday into Tuesday, along with a potentially stronger severe weather setup depending on timing and instability.

For now, enjoy the sunshine and the comfortable temperatures today because summer is quietly starting to rehearse backstage.
